summary: Well-structured product-ideal blueprint that clearly defines the board model and persistence boundary for agent-graph. The durable-vs-transient distinction is explicit and consistently enforced. When/then chains cover the full board lifecycle (open, save, add document, create board, create layer, link chat). usesFiles grounds the blueprint to six concrete implementation files across packages. Minor issues: a bare comment violates Agentish comment rejection, some durability statements appear in both enforces and defines, and failure modes (broken paths, missing documents) are absent.
strengths: Persistence boundary between durable board file and transient runtime state is stated once and reinforced structurally throughout
When/then chains capture six distinct user workflows with clear causality and no ambiguous branching
usesFiles section references concrete implementation paths across agent-graph-core and agent-graph-server, grounding the ideal in real code
Local recoverability is high: a reader with only the language root can fully understand boards, layers, documents, and their relationships
Design opinion is explicit and decisive: new layer is primary action, clone is secondary, graph diff is deferred until workflow is defined
weaknesses: Line 3 bare comment '// Agent Graph Boards' violates the language root's rejection of Comments
Board file durability is stated in both enforces and defines blocks, creating mild redundancy without additional semantic value
No failure-mode or edge-case coverage: missing documents, broken relative paths, or conflicting layer state are unaddressed
Soft language ('intended to live', 'typically under blueprints') introduces interpretive slack in an otherwise decisive file
